title: Acceptance tests are not isolated — share a persistent on-disk DB, suite is non-repeatable
evidence: integration_test.go:107 asserts assert.Len(t, books, 1) for author 'Author A', but tests open the shared file ./books.db (database.go:19 sql.Open("sqlite3","./books.db")) with no reset/cleanup; the archived books.db is non-empty, so a second `go test` run accumulates rows and this assertion fails. main_test.go 'List Books' also depends on the prior 'Create Book' subtest.
suggestion: Use a fresh temp/in-memory DB per test (e.g. an initDB variant taking a DSN, or t.TempDir()); the ATDD prompt requires each scenario to start from an empty service and share no data.

title: PUT /books/{id} on a non-existent id returns 500 instead of 404
evidence: handlers.go:141 updateBook runs UPDATE unconditionally (0 rows affected), then re-SELECTs at handlers.go:150; a missing id yields sql.ErrNoRows which is mapped to http.StatusInternalServerError ('Database error'), never checking RowsAffected for 404.
suggestion: Check result.RowsAffected() after the UPDATE and return 404 when 0, mirroring deleteBook.

title: Error responses are plain text, not JSON, despite the JSON Content-Type header
evidence: handlers.go handlers set w.Header().Set("Content-Type","application/json") then emit errors via http.Error(w, "...", code) which writes text/plain bodies (e.g. handlers.go:86 'Title and author are required', handlers.go:71 'Book not found'). R8 asks for JSON responses.
suggestion: Return errors as JSON objects (e.g. {"error": "..."}) so the body matches the declared content type across all status codes.

title: Validation (title/author required) is implemented but never exercised by a test
evidence: createBook validates at handlers.go:85 and updateBook at handlers.go:135, but no test posts a body missing title/author to assert 400; test_coverage=0.644 (scores.json) leaves the validation/error branches uncovered.
suggestion: Add an acceptance test posting {"author":"x"} (no title) and asserting 400, per ATDD 'reject a book with a missing title'.
